name: Factional Raids
description: Reference entry for Grounded 1 factional raids. It summarizes the attacks on your base that occur when you irritate a faction enough for it to come collect payback.
behavior: Raids do not behave like normal encounters: most creatures prioritize structures, utilities, and soft points in the base before they care about taking a clean duel with you.
interactionWithPlayer: They are triggered by angering specific factions or by forcing attacks with the Waft Emitter. The “Payback Has Arrived!” warning is the sign that you are no longer exploring, but actively defending your base.
interactionWithCreatures: The waves usually share a target and do not fight each other. Some raids also scale with progression, the faction involved, and the real size of the exposed base.
strategy: The general rule is to build with clear lanes, protect utilities, elevate anything that should not touch the ground, and use Guard Dog if you are already playing a lot of defense content. If a ground raid cannot path to the base, it will often collapse on its own.

loot.item: Can contribute to unlocking or upgrading Guard Dog

combatStats.attackDamageSummary: Varies by faction, raid level, and the base being attacked.

abilities.name: Faction Enrage
abilities.description: Killing members, stealing eggs, or invading territory builds enrage until a faction is allowed to attack you.

abilities.name: Payback Has Arrived!
abilities.description: The raid begins with a warning and an approach marker; from there the real job is cutting the path to your base, not chasing pretty kills.

abilities.name: Guard Dog
abilities.description: The Guard Dog mutation boosts damage during base defense and other defense events, so it matters much more here than in normal exploration.

abilities.name: Guard Dog Progress
abilities.description: Defensive event participation can unlock or advance the Guard Dog mutation.

specialTraits: This is not a creature: it summarizes a reactive defense mechanic.
Inside Aphidex this only exists for Grounded 1; Grounded 2 is handled separately.
Aphidex also treats it as a useful entry for tracking Guard Dog progress.
